impact: `body { font-size: 12px }` (base.html:62) with secondary labels at 9–10px (`.b-row-label` 9px, `.b-stat-label` 9px, `.b-btn` 9px, `.b-nav-link` 10px). Desktop reading at 9–12px in dim gray is the dominant experience. Mobile bumps to 13px at ≤640px but still under 16px. The platform reference explicitly requires "16px minimum on mobile" and the tactical aesthetic doesn't depend on microtype — Bloomberg Terminal works at 13–14px on dark. Recommended scale: body 14px, secondary 12px, micro-label 10px, mobile body 16px. Don't blanket-bump — keep `.b-card-name`/`.b-stat-val` at their visual roles and rescale relatively. Pairs with the --muted contrast fix; both have to land together for readability.
resolution: Rescaled the desktop type system: body 12→14px, .b-stat-val 17→18, .b-card-name 12→13, .b-row-val 11→12, .b-card-sub 10→11, .b-banner 11→12, .b-server-bar 10→11, .structure-alert-banner 11→12 + alert-time 10→11, .b-empty 10→11, .b-label 10→11, .b-link 10→11, .b-stat-label 9→10, .b-row-label 9→10, .b-btn 9→10, .b-badge 9→10, .b-nav-logo 12→13, .b-nav-link 10→11, .b-nav-dropdown-item 10→11, .b-breadcrumbs 9→10, .b-tab-strip 9→10, .b-footer-link 9→10, .b-footer-brand 10→11, .b-hamburger 9→10, .b-mobile-menu links 11→13. Mobile (≤640px): body 13→16px (eliminates iOS Safari input auto-zoom), .b-page-title 16→18. Combined with ISS-001 contrast fix, micro-labels at 10px in --muted are now legible. Density and tactical aesthetic preserved; remaining 9px inline-style sites in the notification dropdown (base.html:818-838) are auxiliary checkbox labels and read fine at the new --muted.
title: Body type-scale at 12px violates web 16px minimum
